foundation.title: FILE
foundation.subtitle: Open/Read/Write
foundation.content: Python can read and write to literal files (.txt, .json) on your hard drive. It uses 'open' to create a link to the disk.
foundation.scenario: A Physical File Cabinet. You open it (open), put a new paper inside (write), and then CLOSE the door (close). If you don't close it, the cabinet's drawer gets stuck.

examples.title: Writing
examples.explanation: Creating a new file and putting text inside it instantly.

examples.title: Reading
examples.explanation: Pulling text from a file into a variable.
examples.code: with open('f.txt', 'r') as f: data = f.read()

examples.title: Context
examples.explanation: Using 'with' to automatically close the file if a crash occurs.

foundation.keyPoint: w = write. r = read. a = append.

foundation.title: MODULE
foundation.subtitle: Toolboxes
foundation.content: Modules are pre-built libraries of code. You 'import' them to get new superpowers without writing the logic yourself.
foundation.scenario: A Home Repair Kit. You don't build a screwdriver from scratch. You import (import) the tool from the kit (the module) and use it.

examples.title: Math Lib
examples.explanation: Importing a box of pre-built math tools like Square Root.

examples.title: Time Lib
examples.explanation: Accessing the computer's system clock for timing tasks.

examples.title: Random Lib
examples.explanation: Creating unpredictable numbers for game logic.

foundation.keyPoint: import <module_name> provides its tools to you.

foundation.title: OS_SYS
foundation.subtitle: Talking to Windows
foundation.content: Special modules like 'os' and 'sys' allow your program to talk back to Windows, create folders, and see your system specs.
foundation.scenario: A Control Panel for your PC. Use os to make a folder (mkdir) or list every file in the current directory.

examples.title: List Dir
examples.explanation: Listing every document currently in the folder.

examples.title: Exit Sys
examples.explanation: Instantly killing the program by talking directly to the system.

examples.title: Make Path
examples.explanation: Safe way to build file paths across different versions of Windows.

foundation.keyPoint: os handles files. sys handles the Python interpreter.

gameData.q: Which mode to write to a file?
